ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a grilled fish food product class but no specific entry for a combined grilled fish and tortilla platter. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers grilled fish as a preparation category. No composite platter code exists; closest parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for a grilled fish and tortilla platter composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One platter consisting of approximately 150 g grilled fish fillet served alongside 2–3 corn tortillas (approximately 120 g), salsa, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, and onion, totalling approximately 450 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Fish, tilapia, cooked, dry heat (FDC ID 175177) and Tortillas, ready-to-bake or fry, corn (FDC ID 168898), combined weighted estimate for platter
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a grilled fish with tortilla platter as a composite dish. Values estimated by weighted combination: approximately 33% grilled white fish (tilapia, dry heat, USDA FDC 175177) and 27% corn tortilla (USDA FDC 168898) with 40% salsa/vegetable accompaniments (USDA FDC tomato-based salsa 170457). Per-100g values represent the blended platter composition. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Corn tortilla GI = 52 (Table entry); grilled fish GI effectively negligible (protein/fat dominant); blended platter GI anchored to corn tortilla component.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Corn tortilla has a published GI of 52 (low) per Atkinson et al. 2008 International GI Tables, sourced via University of Sydney GI Database. Grilled fish contributes negligible glycemic load. The platter GI is anchored to the corn tortilla component as the primary carbohydrate source. GL per serving = (GI / 100) × carbohydrate_g_per_serving = (52/100) × (16.5 × 4.5) = 52/100 × 74.25 = 38.6, rounded to 38.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g; a full platter serving (~450 g) delivers approximately 1395 mg sodium, representing 58–70% of the recommended daily limit of 2000–2400 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced-salt preparation, limit or omit added salsa and seasoning, and consider a half-portion to keep sodium intake within safe daily limits.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Corn tortillas provide a moderate glycemic load (GL ~38.7 per platter); the high-protein grilled fish component moderates postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit tortilla portions to one or two pieces, pair with the grilled fish and non-starchy vegetables to blunt glycemic response, and monitor postprandial blood glucose given the moderate overall glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Grilled white fish provides lean protein and omega-3 fatty acids with low saturated fat (~0.7 g per 100 g); preparation by grilling avoids added frying fats.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is generally suitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease when prepared without excessive salt or oil; the lean grilled fish supports a heart-healthy dietary pattern, though sodium from seasoning and salsa should be monitored.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Fish with Tortilla Platter is a Mexican-style main course dish consisting of a seasoned white fish fillet — typically tilapia, snapper, or mahi-mahi — cooked over direct dry heat on a grill until lightly charred and flaky, served alongside two or three warm corn tortillas and a selection of accompaniments such as fresh tomato salsa, shredded lettuce, diced onion, cilantro, and lime wedges. The dish is a staple of Mexican cuisine and is popular in Mexican restaurants globally, including in Singapore where it is offered as a platter meal. The grilled fish delivers a smoky, savoury flavour with a tender, moist interior and lightly crisped exterior. Nutritionally, the dish is a balanced meal providing lean protein from the fish (~12.8 g per 100 g), moderate complex carbohydrates from the corn tortillas, dietary fibre, and micronutrients including vitamin B12, niacin, selenium, and phosphorus. The corn tortilla component contributes a low-to-moderate glycemic index (GI ~52), while the fish itself is essentially glycemically inert. The dish is naturally dairy-free and halal-suitable when prepared without pork-derived ingredients. Clinically, the sodium content from seasoning and salsa warrants attention for hypertensive individuals, and tortilla portion size should be managed for those with Type 2 Diabetes. The lean fish profile makes this dish broadly compatible with cardiovascular-protective dietary patterns.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for a grilled fish with tortilla platter as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted combination of USDA FoodData Central entries for grilled tilapia, corn tortilla, and tomato-based salsa/vegetables. GI is anchored to the published corn tortilla GI of 52 from the University of Sydney GI Database via Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation and restaurant-specific variation in fish species, portion size, and seasoning.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central (FDC IDs 175177, 168898, 170457) weighted by approximate platter composition (33% grilled fish, 27% corn tortilla, 40% salsa/vegetables). Actual values will vary by restaurant, fish species, and preparation. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as grilled white fish and corn tortillas contain negligible industrially produced trans fats; confirmed near-zero per USDA FDC source data. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value estimated at 0.9 g from USDA component data). 4. polyunsaturated_fat_g estimated at 0.8 g from USDA component data. 5. GI of 52 is sourced from Atkinson et al. 2008 International GI Tables for corn tortilla; the platter GI is anchored to this value as the primary carbohydrate source. gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. 6. The dish is served at Fiesta Mexico - Three Amigos Indian and Mexican Restaurant on field survey data at SGD 40.00. 7. Multilingual names provided for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi in accordance with Singapore cuisine multilingual requirements. 8. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not individually reported in the composite USDA source estimation and are documented accordingly; however, in this record all micronutrients have been estimated from component data and no -1 sentinel values remain in the final output.
